articles.title: ASX set to rise, Wall Street mixed; Oil continues to climb
articles.description: The US stock market is drifting in mixed trading, while oil prices climb another 3 per cent as fighting continues in the war with Iran.

articles.title: How will Australia’s emergency services cope if satellites go down? With a sextant and a street directory

articles.title: AusAlert test will blast emergency warning siren over most phones in Australia on Monday. Here’s what to know
articles.description: National emergency test alert will be sent to all mobile devices on 27 July, with domestic violence advocates advising how to turn off safe phones Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast Virtually every phone in the country will blare an emergency siren at 2pm AEST on Monday 27 July as part of the first Australia-wide test of a new national warning system called AusAlert. articles.title: Rusted tin transformed into prize-winning portraits

articles.description: Kiana Elliott thought gymnastics would be her pathway to the podium, but turned to weightlifting after an injury forced her to retire.

articles.description: It might be more than 60 years ago, but Homebush Boys High's 1965 Waratah Shield win remains a fond memory for the likes of Gordon Bray, John Symond and John Coates.

articles.description: The Victorian Coalition pledges $850 million to build a new West Gippsland hospital if it wins the November election.

articles.description: Multiple flights were circling the airport awaiting instructions, while the Bureau of Meteorology issued a warning about the dangerous driving conditions.

articles.description: Donald Trump has issued a new threat to destroy a bridge or power plant every time Iran attacks shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.description: Anthony Albanese may have been a keen participant in robust conference debates in years gone by but is now using his authority to enforce a much more controlled process.

articles.description: One Nation has some of its strongest support in outback Australia and recent polling shows it could win over 30 regional seats. So what's winning over regional Australians when it comes to Pauline Hanson's party?
